1	# Story 5-2: Microsoft Defender for Cloud "Containers" plan validation.
     2	#
     3	# The azurerm provider only exposes `azurerm_security_center_subscription_pricing`
     4	# as a managed resource, with no data-source variant. Story 5-2 must NOT take
     5	# ownership of the subscription Defender plan (AC #6 — subscription scope is
     6	# platform-team owned), so we use the Azure/azapi provider to issue a read-only
     7	# ARM GET against the pricings endpoint and surface the current plan tier via
     8	# a `check` block.
     9	#
    10	# The aliased `azapi.aks` provider (env-level, scoped to var.aks_subscription_id)
    11	# guarantees this read targets and authenticates against the AKS workload
    12	# subscription (operacoes-dev) — not whatever subscription the operator's CLI
    13	# default happens to point to. `parent_id` is built deterministically from
    14	# `var.aks_subscription_id` to keep the URL anchored to the same input that
    15	# scopes the aliased provider, instead of relying on whichever azurerm provider
    16	# the caller threads into this module.
    17	#
    18	# Note: azapi v2 returns `output` as an HCL object (NOT a JSON string) — direct
    19	# dot-access is the supported pattern; do NOT wrap in jsondecode().
    20	#
    21	# Endpoint: GET /subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Security/pricings/Containers?api-version=2024-01-01
    22	# Response shape: { "properties": { "pricingTier": "Free" | "Standard", ... } }
    23	
    24	data "azapi_resource" "defender_containers_pricing" {
    25	  provider = azapi.aks
    26	  type     = "Microsoft.Security/pricings@2024-01-01"
    27	  name     = "Containers"
    28	  # Canonicalize GUID case via `lower()`. The iter-4 validation accepts
    29	  # case-insensitive hex (so Portal-copied uppercase GUIDs pass), but
    30	  # state stores `parent_id` as the literal string — re-applying with a
    31	  # lowercase TF_VAR after a prior uppercase apply would otherwise emit
    32	  # a spurious data-source re-read in CI. ARM is case-insensitive on
    33	  # subscription GUIDs, so lower-casing here has no API effect.
    34	  parent_id              = "/subscriptions/${lower(var.aks_subscription_id)}"
    35	  response_export_values = ["properties.pricingTier"]
    36	}
    37	
    38	# Bind the pricingTier read to a single local so both asserts below
    39	# reference the SAME normalized value. Two-step normalization:
    40	#   1. `try(..., null)` catches errors from attribute access on a null/missing
    41	#      parent and from azapi shape drift that breaks the dotted path; it
    42	#      yields null in those cases.
    43	#   2. The `== null ? "" : try(tostring(...), "")` ternary explicitly
    44	#      coalesces an ARM-returned JSON `null` LEAF value to "" — which
    45	#      `try()` cannot do, because `try()` only swaps on errors, not on
    46	#      successfully-returned null values. The cast itself is wrapped in
    47	#      `try(..., "")` to defend against collection-typed shape drift (an
    48	#      object/list pricingTier would otherwise fail `tostring()` and abort
    49	#      the plan rather than route to the read-path runbook).
    50	# Without this two-step, the value-match assert below can hit
    51	# `lower(null)` and emit the wrong error_message — the exact mis-routing
    52	# iter-3 / iter-4 / iter-5 chased. Rename from `_defender_tier`
    53	# (leading-underscore unidiomatic in HCL) to `defender_pricing_tier`
    54	# matching the project's `policy.tf` local naming convention.
    55	locals {
    56	  # Internal: do not reference outside this file. The normalized value below is the single source of truth.
    57	  defender_pricing_tier_raw = try(data.azapi_resource.defender_containers_pricing.output.properties.pricingTier, null)
    58	  defender_pricing_tier     = local.defender_pricing_tier_raw == null ? "" : try(tostring(local.defender_pricing_tier_raw), "")
    59	}
    60	
    61	# Two asserts: separate the read-success failure mode from the value-match
    62	# failure mode so operators can tell which lane to debug. The read-success
    63	# assert requires a non-empty `pricingTier` — null OR empty-string both fail

    65	# Defender-tier-deviation runbook (an empty enum from ARM is a read-path bug,
    66	# not a plan-tier deviation). The value assertion short-circuits when
    67	# pricingTier is null/empty (delegating that case to the read-success assert)
    68	# so an empty-enum response surfaces exactly ONE warning, not two — see the
    69	# 2026-05-12 review finding on Terraform `check` assert non-short-circuiting.
    70	# The value comparison is case-insensitive (ARM has historically returned
    71	# mixed-case enums in preview/region variants) and tolerates a missing
    72	# property by coercing to "missing" before comparing.

    80	        (a) `TF_VAR_aks_subscription_id` resolves to operacoes-dev (echo it and confirm the GUID).
    81	        (b) The principal authenticated by `azapi.aks` has `Microsoft.Security/pricings/read` on that subscription.
    82	        (c) The read isn't being blocked by a transient ARM error or a 404 on a never-initialized pricing record.

    86	              # PowerShell 5.1/7 (TF_VAR_aks_subscription_id exported into $env):
